stem: In the figure, \(DE\) is parallel to \(BC\). If \(AD=4\), \(DB=2\), and \(DE=6\), what is the length of \(BC\)?
solutionStandard: Since \(DE \parallel BC\), triangles \(ADE\) and \(ABC\) are similar, with ratio \(AD:AB = 4:6=2:3\). So \(DE:BC=2:3\), giving \(BC = 6 \times \frac{3}{2} = 9\).

stem: Triangle A has sides 3, 4, 5. Triangle B is similar to Triangle A with a scale factor of 3. What is the length of the longest side of Triangle B?
solutionStandard: The longest side of Triangle A is 5; scaling by 3 gives \(5 \times 3 = 15\).

stem: Two similar triangles have corresponding sides in ratio 2:5.
quantityA: The ratio of their areas, as a fraction (smaller to larger)
solutionStandard: The area ratio is the square of the side ratio: \(\left(\dfrac{2}{5}\right)^2 = \dfrac{4}{25} = 0.16\). Since \(2/5=0.4\) and \(0.16 < 0.4\), Quantity B is greater.

stem: Triangle \(ABC\) is similar to triangle \(DEF\), with \(AB=6\), \(DE=9\), and the area of \(ABC\) is 16. What is the area of \(DEF\)?
solutionStandard: Side ratio \(AB:DE = 6:9=2:3\). Area ratio \(=2^2:3^2=4:9\). Area of \(DEF = 16 \times \frac{9}{4}=36\).

stem: A tree casts a shadow 24 feet long at the same time a 6-foot-tall person casts a shadow 4 feet long. How tall is the tree, in feet?
solutionStandard: The triangles formed by the person/shadow and tree/shadow are similar: \(\dfrac{6}{4} = \dfrac{h}{24}\), so \(h = \dfrac{6 \times 24}{4} = 36\).

stem: In triangle \(ABC\), \(D\) is on \(AB\) and \(E\) is on \(AC\) such that \(DE \parallel BC\). If \(AD=3\), \(AB=8\), and the area of triangle \(ADE\) is 9, what is the area of triangle \(ABC\)?
solutionStandard: Side ratio \(AD:AB=3:8\), so area ratio \(\text{Area}_{ADE}:\text{Area}_{ABC} = 9:64\). Since \(\text{Area}_{ADE}=9\), we get \(\text{Area}_{ABC}=64\).

stem: Which of the following conditions guarantee that two triangles are similar? Select all that apply.

options.text: All three pairs of corresponding angles are equal
Two pairs of corresponding angles are equal
All three pairs of corresponding sides are proportional
One pair of corresponding sides is equal

solutionStandard: (A) is the definition of similarity. (B) is sufficient because the third angle is then automatically determined (angles sum to 180°). (C) (side-side-side proportionality) also guarantees similarity. (D) alone says nothing about shape.

stem: Triangle \(ABC\) has a right angle at \(B\). \(D\) is on \(AC\) such that \(BD\) is perpendicular to \(AC\). If \(AD=4\) and \(DC=9\), what is the length of \(BD\)?
solutionStandard: The altitude to the hypotenuse creates two triangles similar to the original and to each other, giving \(BD^2 = AD \times DC = 4 \times 9 = 36\), so \(BD=6\).

stem: Two similar triangles have areas 25 and 64. If the perimeter of the smaller triangle is 20, what is the perimeter of the larger triangle?
solutionStandard: Area ratio \(25:64\) means side (and perimeter) ratio \(\sqrt{25}:\sqrt{64}=5:8\). Perimeter of larger \(= 20 \times \dfrac{8}{5}=32\).

stem: Triangle \(ABC\) is similar to triangle \(DEF\) with a scale factor of \(\frac{3}{2}\) (\(ABC\) is larger).
quantityA: The ratio of the perimeter of \(ABC\) to the perimeter of \(DEF\)
quantityB: The ratio of the area of \(ABC\) to the area of \(DEF\)
solutionStandard: Perimeter ratio equals the scale factor: \(3/2=1.5\). Area ratio is the square of the scale factor: \((3/2)^2=9/4=2.25\). Since \(2.25>1.5\), Quantity B is greater.
